violations.text: The doors to the outside, located at the store entrance, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2025

The doors to the outside, located at the store entrance, are not tight-fitting. Space observed at the bottom between the doors. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 10/10/2024

Ambient temperature of the deli/dairy reach-in cold hold observed fluctuating between 42- and 45-degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods evaluated and deemed to be safe. White cardboard observed to be lining the shelves in these units. This cardboard must be removed as it will interfere with air flow. Service call to be placed and units to be carefully monitored for food safety. New Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2023

Thermometers are lacking in the deli/dairy refrigeration unit(s). Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 05/17/2023
Deli/dairy refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair tearing away from the doors. New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2023
